response.justification: The candidate lacks direct, sustained C++ experience on Windows in capital markets or fixed-income domains, with most C++ work being academic or non-financial (e.g., bachelor’s thesis, LabWindows), and primary expertise centered on JavaScript/TypeScript, DLang, and web/mobile stacks — failing to meet the mandatory 5–10 years of C++ in financial systems context.
strengths: Strong full-stack and polyglot development skills across multiple languages and platforms
Demonstrated experience with CI/CD, testing practices, and dev-ops tooling
Active open-source contributor and technical educator with clear communication skills
concerns: Minimal evidence of professional C++ development on Windows in financial domains
No clear exposure to fixed income, derivatives, or investment banking systems
SQL experience listed but unverified in enterprise financial database contexts (SQL Server/Oracle)
